result.question:
For the WAGER project we build executable "worlds" where a specific cognitive operation is gated behind the top score. We already have a catalog of investigative-JUDGMENT VICES (each vice a losing move). We are now building the MIRROR catalog: the POSITIVE creative operations of scientific discovery — "aha moments" — where PERFORMING the creative operation is the only path to the ceiling. CRUCIAL CONSTRAINT: our worlds are scored with a NUMBER, no LLM judge, so an aha counts for us ONLY if the insight PAYS OFF IN A MEASURABLE PREDICTION (e.g., recognizing that system A and system B share a deep structure lets you predict B's behavior you otherwise couldn't).

Find the STRUCTURES of aha moments / creative cognitive operations in scientific discovery, from BOTH the cognitive science of discovery AND the history of science. Anchor in the formal literature: Dedre Gentner's structure-mapping theory of analogy; Kevin Dunbar's in-vivo studies; Nancy Nersessian's model-based reasoning; Paul Thagard's conceptual revolution / explanatory coherence / conceptual combination; Holyoak & Thagard; Herbert Simon & David Klahr (scientific discovery as dual-space search, BACON); Margaret Boden's types of creativity; Hofstadter & Mitchell (Copycat); William Whewell's consilience; C.S. Peirce's abduction.

FOR EACH structure, report it as a PAIR — this is essential, the design unit is the pair, not the lone aha:
(a) THE OPERATION — the abstract cognitive mechanism, described concretely enough to rebuild as a synthetic world.
(b) GENIUS CASE — a concrete documented case where the operation produced a real discovery (author/case, year).
(c) DOCUMENTED MISUSE / EVIL TWIN — a concrete documented case where the SAME operation was applied and was WRONG (the vice pole). Example pairings: structural analogy ↔ false/surface-similarity analogy; unification/consilience ↔ apophenia/spurious pattern; splitting-a-concept-in-two ↔ needless multiplication of entities; positing a hidden entity that pays off (Neptune, neutrino, Mendel's genes) ↔ positing an ad-hoc unobservable that never pays off (N-rays, epicycles, luminiferous ether); promoting a real anomaly ↔ chasing noise; persisting rightly ↔ tunnel-vision. Give the misuse case WITH a source.
(d) TIER — A = documented in LLMs/AI agents with a citation; B = human-only paradigm (cognitive psychology / history of science) not yet tested in LLMs. Mark EVERY structure with its tier.
(e) PAYOFF TYPE — does the insight reduce to a MEASURABLE PREDICTION (buildable in a zero-LLM predict-the-hidden-mechanism world) or only to comprehension/elegance (NOT numerically scoreable)? For re-representation / representational change specifically, DISTINGUISH two sub-cases: (i) a new representation that UNLOCKS a measurable prediction — e.g., a pattern invisible in the raw variables but obvious in a derived one (a ratio, a rate, a cumulative sum) — which IS buildable and whose observable signature is that the solution constructs a variable nobody handed it; from (ii) pure comprehensive elegance with no prediction, which is NOT buildable in our scoring.

Group by structure. Prioritize concrete cases at BOTH poles (genius and misuse), structural diversity, and quantified findings over generic commentary.

I ALREADY HAVE (do NOT re-report): the basic Darwin natural-selection structural-isomorphism example; Dunbar's near-analogy dominance, unitary→two-mechanism conceptual split, and anomaly-attention gated by centrality×timing; the Klayman-Ha subset/superset hypothesis geometry. Find ADDITIONAL structures — and above all their MISUSE twins and the tier + payoff classification for each.
result.summary: The literature yields a small set of reusable "aha" STRUCTURES, each of which is a genuine pair — a positive creative operation and a documented misuse pole — and, crucially for WAGER, most of them reduce to a MEASURABLE prediction rather than mere elegance. The strongest buildable structures are: (1) structure-mapping analogy, whose payoff is inference-projection (predicates in the base but not the target are projected as testable candidate predictions), with a well-quantified evil twin (surface-similarity retrieval, 70% vs 30%) that is now also documented in LLMs (Tier A: GPT accuracy collapses on counterfactual analogy variants while humans stay high); (2) consilience/unification, which Thagard formalizes as a COUNTABLE quantity (facts explained) and implements as a running zero-LLM constraint network (ECHO: winner WH1 = +0.94 vs loser PH1 = -0.85), with the wave-theory-of-light case (Poisson spot, conical refraction) as the genius exemplar; (3) dual-space search (SDDS), where the ceiling requires moving between hypothesis space and experiment space, not just generating hypotheses; and (4) representational change that pays off — the Klahr & Dunbar counter→selector reframe, a discontinuous ≥3-slot jump that unlocks correct prediction of a device's behavior, which is exactly the buildable sub-case (i) where a new representation unlocks a measurable prediction. Copycat's conceptual slippage supplies the mechanism-level account of why the same re-representation machinery forks into deep-structural (genius) vs surface (evil-twin) mappings. Tier is mostly B (human paradigms not yet tested in LLMs) EXCEPT analogy, whose misuse pole is now Tier-A documented in GPT models.

findings.claim: STRUCTURE — Structure-mapping analogy as a PREDICTION-generating operation. (a) OPERATION: align two systems on higher-order relational structure (systematicity bias: prefer systems of relations bound by causal/mathematical constraints over isolated relations), then project predicates present in the base but not yet in the target as candidate inferences (inference-projection / structural completion). The buildable signature: the solver predicts a hidden target predicate it was never handed, licensed only by the shared relational skeleton. (b) GENIUS CASE: Darwin's artificial-selection→natural-selection mapping, formally modeled as analogy links (Thagard ECHO Principle 3) that boost the natural-selection hypotheses' activation. (c) EVIL TWIN: surface-similarity-driven retrieval — people retrieve/use analogies on surface features not relational structure (Trench & Minervino 2015: 70% retrieval in high-surface condition vs 30% in low-surface), so deep analogies are missed and surface matches dominate; the failure space is itself a taxonomy of four types (false analogy, misanalogy, disanalogy, counteranalogy — Shelley 2003, along orientation×effect). (d) TIER: B for the human operation; but its misuse pole is ALSO Tier A (see LLM finding). (e) PAYOFF: MEASURABLE — inference-projection yields a concrete candidate prediction about the target; buildable in a zero-LLM predict-the-hidden-mechanism world.
findings.evidence: Gentner's OECS 'Analogy' entry (primary) gives inference-projection ('predicates connected to the common system but not present in the target are projected as candidate inferences') and the systematicity bias ('prefer to map systems of relations connected by higher-order constraining relations'). Trench & Minervino (2015, Cognitive Science 39) supply the 70%/30% surface-retrieval quantification — one of the most replicated findings in the analogy literature. Thagard (1989 BBS) Principle 3 (Analogy) verbatim models Darwin's argument as analogy links boosting activation. Shelley (2003, Argumentation 17:141-166) gives the 2x2 four-failure-type taxonomy.

findings.claim: TIER-A EVIL TWIN (LLM-documented) — surface-similarity analogy masquerading as structure-mapping, now demonstrated in GPT models. LLMs perform well on default/in-distribution letter-string, digit-matrix, and story analogies but accuracy COLLAPSES on counterfactual/perturbed variants (permuted alphabets, non-letter symbol strings) while human accuracy stays high. Brittleness generalizes across formats: answer-order effects and heightened paraphrase sensitivity that humans do not show. The authors conclude LLMs lack the robustness/generality of zero-shot human analogy-making — i.e., a brittle pattern-match bound to training distribution rather than genuine abstract structure-mapping. This is the concrete Tier-A vice pole a WAGER world can gate against.
findings.evidence: Lewis & Mitchell (2024), 'Evaluating the Robustness of Analogical Reasoning in LLMs' (arXiv:2411.14215) and the companion counterfactual-tasks paper (arXiv:2402.08955): 'while the performance of humans remains high for all the problems, the GPT models' performance declines sharply on the counterfactual set' and LLMs 'lack the robustness and generality of human analogy-making.' Note an active counter-literature (Webb/Holyoak/Lu; a 2025 PNAS Nexus rebuttal) argues for emergent analogical reasoning — so this is the authors' documented finding, not settled consensus, but the specific empirical divergence on variants is robust.

findings.claim: STRUCTURE — Consilience / unification as a COUNTABLE payoff. (a) OPERATION: a hypothesis explains a class of facts DIFFERENT IN KIND from those it was originally induced from, without ad hoc adjustment (Whewell's consilience of inductions); coherence/acceptability rise monotonically with the number of facts explained. Thagard makes this the single most important criterion for best explanation. Decomposes into two processes, only the second (explanatory theory undergoes conceptual 'stretching') being genuine consilience, which confers 'extraordinarily high support.' (b) GENIUS CASE: the wave theory of light — it explained phenomena the particle theory could not (E7 the Poisson/Arago central bright spot, conical refraction, interference of polarized light), giving it more 'Perfect' explanations. (c) EVIL TWIN: apophenia / spurious unification (a theory that appears to explain heterogeneous facts by ad-hoc stretching) — this pole is theoretically implied (non-consilient case with low support) but NO sourced documented misuse case survived verification; a gap to fill. (d) TIER: B. (e) PAYOFF: MEASURABLE — score by counting distinct evidence propositions the winning principle covers; the genius operation reduces to explanatory breadth, a number.
findings.evidence: Thagard (1989 BBS): 'the more a hypothesis explains, the more coherent and hence acceptable it is... this principle subsumes the criterion of explanatory breadth (which Whewell called consilience)... the most important for selecting the best explanation.' Fisch (1985, Philosophy of Science 52(2)): Whewellian consilience 'differs considerably' from loose modern usage, decomposes into two processes, and 'in the event of genuine consilience... a theory acquires extraordinarily high support.' Thagard's waves paper uses Powell's 1833 Perfect/Imperfect/None table with E7 = 'Light has a central spot' (Poisson spot). Caveat: Thagard attributes the wave-theory win to breadth PLUS simplicity PLUS analogy, so raw count slightly oversimplifies.

findings.claim: BUILDABILITY PROOF — explanatory coherence / IBE is computable as a NUMBER with zero LLM. ECHO partitions propositions into accepted/rejected sets by maximizing satisfaction of positive (explanatory) and negative (contradiction/competition) constraints, settling a connectionist network to real-valued activations that decide the winner (wave WH1 = +0.94 accepted, particle PH1 = -0.85 rejected). Complementary structural point: Inference to the Best Explanation is a TWO-STEP operation — abduction generates/proposes the candidate hypothesis (the creative act), a distinct second step evaluates its explanatory power (the score). This cleanly separates the generative aha from its numerical scoring, which is exactly the WAGER contract: the world gates the ceiling behind the generative operation and scores it by the settled coherence number.
findings.evidence: Thagard waves paper (primary): 'A simple connectionist algorithm spreads activation among the propositions until some are accepted (activation greater than 0) and others are rejected'; Table 2 shows WH1=0.94, PH1=-0.85. ECHO is a canonical 1989-1998 connectionist model with NO LLM component. Synthese (2011, DOI 10.1007/s11229-011-0054-y): 'abduction [is] the first step of IBE. The second step amounts to the evaluation of explanatory power.' The two-step generation-vs-evaluation split is mainstream (corroborated by SEP abduction entry).

findings.claim: STRUCTURE — Dual-space search (SDDS). (a) OPERATION: scientific reasoning is coordinated search in TWO problem spaces — a hypothesis space (searched by retrieving/recombining prior knowledge from memory) and an experiment space (searched by generalizing from experimental results). The ceiling requires MOVING BETWEEN spaces, not just generating hypotheses. (b) GENIUS CASE: Klahr & Dunbar's BigTrak studies — 13 of 20 subjects ('Experimenters') induced the correct frame from experiments in a specific informative region (region III) that rules out all common hypotheses and is consistent only with the true rule; in Study 2, subjects who could not retrieve the rule from memory found it ONLY by generalizing from experiments. (c) EVIL TWIN: getting trapped searching one space — pure hypothesis-space (memory) search that never designs the discriminating experiment (the non-Experimenters who could not cross over). (d) TIER: B. (e) PAYOFF: MEASURABLE — the payoff comes from designing the discriminating experiment (reaching region III); buildable as a world where the winning move is an experiment choice that discriminates hypotheses, scored by whether the chosen experiment lands in the informative region.
findings.evidence: Klahr & Dunbar (1988, Cognitive Science 12(1):1-48, DOI 10.1207/s15516709cog1201_1), the canonical SDDS paper: 'search in two problem spaces (an hypothesis space and an experiment space) shapes hypothesis generation, experimental design, and the evaluation of hypotheses.' Verbatim: 'Thirteen subjects were classified as Experimenters'; region III programs 'rule out all the common hypotheses and are consistent only with HS1.' Study 2: 'Subjects who could not think of the correct rule in the hypothesis generation phase discovered the correct rule only by generalizing from the results of experiments.' BigTrak is a deterministic microworld scored on binary correctness — directly zero-LLM buildable.

findings.claim: STRUCTURE — Representational change that PAYS OFF (the buildable sub-case i). (a) OPERATION: a discontinuous reframe of the raw variables into a new representation that unlocks a prediction invisible in the original framing. Concretely, Klahr & Dunbar's subjects reconceived a device's N parameter from a 'counter' to a 'selector'; crossing frames required changing at least three representational slots SIMULTANEOUSLY (N-role, unit-of-repetition, number-of-repetitions, bounds), which is why incremental hypothesis-space revision could not reach it — a discontinuous multi-slot jump. (b) GENIUS CASE: once subjects switched N-role counter→selector and built the new frame, 'they quickly discovered how the RPT key works' — the reframe directly unlocked correct prediction of device behavior. (c) EVIL TWIN: staying in the wrong frame (17/20 subjects started in the counter frame and could not predict) — tunnel-vision on the initial representation. (d) TIER: B. (e) PAYOFF: MEASURABLE and buildable — this is precisely sub-case (i): the observable signature is that the solution CONSTRUCTS a variable nobody handed it (the selector reframe / the derived slot configuration), after which a previously-unpredictable output becomes predictable. NOT the pure-elegance sub-case (ii). The world gates the ceiling behind a discontinuous multi-slot re-representation.
findings.evidence: Klahr & Dunbar (1988), verbatim from the published PDF: 'The most significant representational change occurred when N-role was switched from counter to selector and a new frame was constructed. Once subjects made this change, they quickly discovered how the RPT key works.' And p.20: 'whenever there is a shift from one frame to the other, at least three slots must change value simultaneously.' The four-slot frame (N-role, unit-of-repetition, number-of-repetitions, bounds) with two subsidiary frames (counter, selector) is explicit; 17/20 started in the wrong frame. The discontinuous-jump design gloss is a sound structural inference (a single incremental slot revision cannot cross frames when ≥3 slots must change).

findings.claim: STRUCTURE (mechanism-level) — Copycat conceptual slippage: WHY re-representation forks into genius vs evil twin. (a) OPERATION: analogy-making modeled as high-level/abstract PERCEPTION — prior concepts are activated by a situation and feed back to CHANGE how that situation is perceived (re-representation of the input), rather than matching over fixed features. Under contextual pressure a concept 'slips' into a neighboring role/concept, which is what lets a deeper structural mapping replace a surface one. (b) GENIUS CASE: in 'aabc:aabd::ijkk:ijll' the rightmost single letter's role slips to the rightmost GROUP because 'aa' and 'kk' play similar roles — the deep structural answer. (c) EVIL TWIN: the SAME slippage/temperature machinery can settle on a shallow surface correspondence instead of the deep one — the genius/misuse fork lives in one mechanism (Copycat encodes 'conceptual depth': deep concepts resist slippage, so a settling that over-slips yields the surface answer). (d) TIER: B (fixed 1990s model; not the modern LLM test). (e) PAYOFF: this is the OPERATION-level account for the representational-change structure above; its own outputs are discrete answers (measurable), but its primary WAGER value is explaining the re-representation signature — the solver reconstructs the situation before it can answer.
findings.evidence: Hofstadter & Mitchell, Copycat (primary; Mitchell's MIT Press book is literally titled 'Analogy-Making as Perception'): 'analogy-making should be modeled as a process of abstract perception, in which one's prior concepts are activated by a situation and feed back to affect how that situation is perceived.' The 'aabc:aabd::ijkk:ijll' slippage example and the Slipnet 'conceptual depth' feature (deep concepts resistant to slippage) are documented; Copycat was explicitly a critique of fixed-feature matching. The evil-twin framing (same machinery → shallow vs deep) faithfully restates the documented surface-vs-deep quality distinction.

result.caveats: TIER SKEW: Only the analogy structure has a Tier-A (LLM-documented) misuse pole (Lewis & Mitchell 2024); every other structure here is Tier B (human cognitive-psych / history-of-science paradigm not yet tested in LLMs). Do not overstate LLM evidence for consilience, dual-space search, or representational change — those are human-only so far. MISSING MISUSE CASES: The verified claims did NOT deliver a SOURCED documented misuse case for consilience (the apophenia/spurious-unification 'evil twin' is only theoretically implied by the non-consilient low-support case in Fisch, not a named historical example). Likewise, the requested hidden-entity pair (Neptune/neutrino/Mendel's genes ↔ N-rays/epicycles/ether), the concept-splitting ↔ multiplication-of-entities pair, and the anomaly-promotion ↔ noise-chasing pair did NOT surface in the 23 confirmed claims at all — they remain unbuilt for this batch. SOURCE QUALITY: Strong throughout — nearly all primary (Gentner's own OECS entry, Thagard's own BBS paper and code, the actual Klahr & Dunbar 1988 paper verified from the published PDF, Hofstadter/Mitchell primary, Lewis & Mitchell arXiv). Two claims were 2-1 (claim 10's specific '15+ Perfect' tally is a reviewer interpolation not stated in Thagard; claim 21). CONTESTED FIELD: LLM analogy robustness is actively debated — Webb/Holyoak/Lu and a 2025 PNAS Nexus paper argue for emergent analogical reasoning; treat the brittleness result as the authors' well-documented finding, not settled consensus. ELEGANCE-ONLY RISK: Copycat's value is mechanism-explanation; its own scoreable outputs are discrete but thin — its main WAGER use is explaining the re-representation signature, not standing alone as a world.
openQuestions: The hidden-entity pair the task explicitly wanted (positing an unobservable that PAYS OFF — Neptune, neutrino, Mendel's genes — vs an ad-hoc unobservable that never pays off — N-rays, epicycles, luminiferous ether) did not appear in any confirmed claim. It is arguably the single most WAGER-buildable structure (predict-the-hidden-mechanism is native to it) and needs a dedicated search with sourced cases at BOTH poles.

Consilience's evil twin needs a NAMED, sourced apophenia / spurious-unification case (e.g., a historical over-unifying theory that counted coincidences as explanations). Fisch gives the theory of low-support non-consilience but not a concrete misused example — required to build the vice pole.

Are dual-space search, representational change, and consilience testable in LLMs (to promote them from Tier B to Tier A)? No LLM study surfaced for these; whether GPT/Claude fail specifically at cross-space movement or at multi-slot reframing is an open, buildable empirical question that WAGER worlds could themselves answer.

For the ECHO/coherence scoring: since Thagard attributes theory selection to breadth PLUS simplicity PLUS analogy jointly, does a pure count-of-facts-explained metric adequately isolate the consilience 'aha', or does the world need to also penalize ad-hoc auxiliary hypotheses (simplicity) to avoid rewarding spurious over-explanation?

refuted.claim: These four analogy-failure modes are grounded in Holyoak & Thagard's (1995) multiconstraint theory of analogy (structural consistency, semantic/similarity, and pragmatic constraints), meaning each misuse type corresponds to the violation of a specific constraint that a good structural mapping must satisfy — useful for designing distinct 'vice poles' against the structural-analogy aha.

Advanced LLMs (GPT-4, Claude 3) match human accuracy on semantic structure-mapping analogy tasks in most conditions, with no significant difference from humans in the default condition (GPT-4 p=0.097, Claude 3 p=0.194), providing Tier-A evidence that Gentner's structure-mapping relational-analogy operation is performed by LLMs.
